item.title: "Education is learning how to spot the signs of history repeating itself" -Noam Chomsky
item.description: While I haven't read all of his books, I have yet to read one that I didn't find incredibly insightful. He is definitely one of our great thinkers of this period of time. *Edited to add* He's not perfect. He has gotten things horribly wrong in the past and had to take accountability. Some recent comments of his have definitely missed the mark. However, none of that invalidates the message of this particular quote.
item.dc:creator: GwenGifTerror

item.title: While our congress screws balks at sending more arms to Ukraine...
item.description:
Okay, math quiz: Which is a higher price? A few billion in arms for Ukraine or the life of ONE American soldier (among thousands!) who would be fighting in Europe when Putin (as he has already stated) attacks the Balkans, Germany, and the rest of NATO? Now call and or write your congressmen and senators and tell them what you figured out (and what those morons already know, but don't care about). Demand that we continue to support Ukraine as they defend Europe and their own sovereign nation.
Sorry for the messed up title. Tired and sick this week...
item.dc:creator: SonsofLibertyArise

item.dc:creator: ThePocho361

item.title: It cost zero dollars to mind your own business
item.description: If you have SNAP you can get Amazon Prime for $4/month. 2 years ago I was out of work for a while and had SNAP assistance for a few months. If you add the snap card to an Amazon prime account it permanently reduces the cost of prime to $4/month (card has to be active with a balance when added to the account) even if the Snap card doesn't have a balance or is expired. They never check it. This is full prime with Amazon video. FP Edit: If you are financially in a bad spot please check out findhelp.org You put in your zip code and it pulls up local charities and non profits that can help people in your situation, things like paying your utilities bills, groceries, help with housing and internet. It's an incredibly good resource.
item.dc:creator: Sensiblyinteresting

item.title: There's no sock like horse sock.
item.description: Found the weird black thing at a thrift store and had NO CLUE what it was really for beyond my gutter brained idea, but then saw a viral post with it in it, and it mentioned compression socks. FINALLY a clue to go on! Honestly, actually pretty cool. You use the giant hard plastic... ROD to hold the sock, then you take this soft... SLEEVE and you roll it down the rod all the way to the ba- You know what, just watch the video instead. Pay no attention to those lyrics though.
item.dc:creator: Onlyhereforthelaughs

item.dc:creator: BPhotographyZt

item.title: I spent part of my summer this year retracing as many real world locations from Assassin's Creed. I photographed the exact same places from the games and in real life side by side to see how well they compare.
item.description:
The Great Pyramid of Khufu viewed from the south. The tallest man man made structure for 4 millennia. When it was completed in 2570 BCE, woolly mammoths still roamed in Siberia
The dome of The Cathedral of Santa Maria del Fiore from atop the bell tower in ACII & the picture I took from the same perspective IRL. The architect of the dome is buried inside.
The Colossi of Memnon are giant statues of Amenhotep III that lie on the Niles West Bank. Destroyed in an earthquake, they have been reconstructed but appear shattered & broken. AC O depicts their look before damage
View of the Doge's Palace from the Venice lagoon in ACII and IRL. Did not spot a single sail ship anymore...
These decorative cobra Friezes towards the end of the Stepped Pyramid complex are immaculately recreated in the game. I was just stunned that Ubisoft got this small detail so right because no one would have noticed if they never put it in, but having seen it up close IRL and in the game made my head spin a bit for how accurate it was.
Pop! Ezio in front of the East End of the Duomo di Firenze and the same place inside the game.
The Giza Necropolis from the southern panoramic edge. Giza is perhaps the single most visited tourist site on the African Continent fascinating humanity for 4,500 years
The view from atop St Mark's Campanile of St Marks Basilica IRL and in AC II. Funny how the red brick tones and grey colors are also an exact match.
A Sphinx in front of the Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut IRL and inside ACO to the bottom. The game has a small avenue of sphinxs there but today only the one remains. The one IRL has the head of a person but the one in the game was replaced with a flacon
Bridge near St Marks Square. Retracing my steps inside the game & I found the same spot. Even though ACII isn't an exact 1:1 recreation, I was impressed I could still find it
An exterior wide view of the Temple of Amun as depicted in AC O and the photo I took of what it looks like today.
Front façade of Saint Mark's Basilica in Venice. Amazing detailed recreation by Ubisoft here. The glass panes are only black because of the sun and light, otherwise are still original color
Statues of Queen Hatshepsut line the columns of her temple in The Valley of the Queens. One of few Female Pharaohs she is famously depicted in male drag to reinforce the appearance of her legitimacy to Egypt
Giotto's Bell Tower in front of the Domo in Florence from ACII and IRL. Recreating the EXACT angle of this in game was impossible because of the condensed area in front of it in ACII
Standing outside the eastern exterior of the Temple of Amun in Siwa Oasis. Time has not been kind to the temple but after thousands of years it still calls people to visit Siwa
Viewing the Grand Canal from atop St Mark's Campanile
Snefrus Bent Pyramid in Dashur was built 40 years before the Giza Complex & is located 25 miles south of Giza. It was an early attempt at a real pyramid but was a botched attempt
Palazzo Vecchio where Ezio's dad was imprisoned, & where the conspirator Drancesco de' Pazzi was killed when he was hung from its parapets for the killing of Giuliano de Medici
Western Door of the Temple of Karnak in Thebes (ModernDay Luxor) & what remains of it today. Just the doorway & the base of some of the columns.
Cellini's iconic statue of Perseus in Piazza della Signoria IRL & in ACII. They made it marble in the game instead of bronze. Today its in a Medici alcove directly left of this
A statue of The Hidden One: God Amun I found. This statue was photographed inside the Temple of Karnak & it's counter part can be found inside the game as well
Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut IRL and in AC O. I noticed they made it less wider and took away many columns but the basic structure is still the same with the giant ramp leading in
The old bridge of Florence or "Ponte Vecchio" in Italian. It was a very bright day in Firenze that day so the colors pop more IRL
Several rams head sphinxs line the entrance to the Temple of Luxor in Thebes & the same also line the entrance to Karnak temple IRL. Ubisoft switched the two entrances appearances
View of the Sanctuary inside the Duomo di Firenze in IRL & in ACII. ACII did put in some sky portals in the central dome but are out of frame in this shot when I matched it. Also in the game the ceiling wasn't painted like it is today because the game takes place nearly a century before it as actually painted, so the bare look on the right is historically accurate for the year AC II is set in
Standing at the entrance to Saqqara Complex IRL & Bayek in the exact same spot. Interesting how in the game they widened the structure. The decorative indentations are much closer IRL
A side view of one of the mastabas at the foot of the Stepped Pyramid. The deified and famous Egyptian Architect Imhotep is said to be responsible for the building of this complex
View of the Duomo & Palazzo Vecchio tower from near the Arno river in Florence Italy & the same place IRL. Obviously the real Vecchio tower is much taller. I didn't have time to go up there sadly.
Looking upwards at the Bent Pyramid from the East End of the site. The remnants of a sacred edifice remain on its eastern side today.
Ezio standing in front of Botticelli's renaissance masterpiece "The Birth of Venus" in ACII & the original painting in the Uffizi Gallery in Firenze *Forgot to bring Pop! Ezio here
The north side of the Great Pyramid of Khufu. The Chevron façade decorating the entrance is still visible to this day. Tourists over the centuries have damaged it with graffiti.
Viewing the Dome of the Basilica di San Lorenzo from streets of Florence & the same perspective IRL. That building is where the Medici are entombed.
View of the Sphinx from the south and the remnants of a mastaba next to it. Mastabas were funerary edifices for the less important members of the Pharaohs inner circle.
Exploring Giza on camel. The Pyramid of Khafre artificially looks bigger than Khufu's Pyramid (Khafre's dad) since it was intentionally built on higher ground but Khufu's is larger
item.dc:creator: WildYucatanMan

it's surprisingly light for its size! (~2 kg)
closer look

item.dc:creator: epicinternetgf

item.title: Did you know pilots take naps while you're in the back eating snacks thinking they fly the plane ?
item.description: This is called a controlled rest. This is done by reclining the seat and there are procedures in place to make sure everything is safe. Imagine flying a 6 hour flight overnight without sleeping, that is much more dangerous, so this procedure was introduced. Normally pilots take turns napping, and they have a certain period of time after they wake up where they are not allowed to touch the controls. Totally safe and normal.
item.dc:creator: ImpatientMoth

item.title: Atacama desert after rain
item.dc:creator: MickLeafhill

item.title: Completely Devastated
item.description:
Thought I'd share with people who can appreciate what my memorabilia collection meant to me. Initially posted on Reddit, but I lurk Imgur as well.
There was a fire in the storage facility where I had a unit. Here is where I kept all my collectibles and memorabilia related to blink-182; it was a total loss.
The CDs are just what didn't get entirely destroyed beyond recognition, and I was able to get some closure with them.
The insurance has already paid out my claim, except some of those items were invaluable to me.
Some of the jewels of my collection were a drum head signed by all four members of +44, as well as a promotional skate deck which was also signed. A drumstick Travis used in their second show at SOMA in San Diego, a signed ticket, and a signed T-shirt; all from that same night. Along with every version released of WYHSB album, the green version is the only one that survived.
I'm really bummed out about the whole situation, not the best way to end 2023.
This was from the Simple Creatures meet-and-greet at their show in San Francisco (can't recall the date exactly) #blink182 #plus44 #AVA #MarkHoppus #TravisBarker #TomDeLonge
item.dc:creator: EctoJuan8
